title: resolution order: specific>wildcard-action>unlabeled>wildcard-source>rejection
manager: The single, total precedence that decides which transition fires when several could match: specific beats wildcard-action beats unlabeled beats wildcard-source beats rejection. This is the rule that makes wildcards, unlabeled edges, and specific transitions coexist predictably — without it, a machine with overlapping edges would be a guessing game about which arrow wins. It is the graph's definitive answer to "which transition applies."
cs: total resolution order specific > wildcard-action > unlabeled > wildcard-source > rejection; deterministic edge selection; consumes the multigraph rules (W6.87) and wildcards (W6.88).
